mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects aggregate income expansion and consumer spending power, which drives corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not significantly positively correlated with month-over-month PAYEMS changes (Rank IC <= 0.02) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a trailing three-month window signals weakening labor demand that precedes earnings downgrades and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6% as of Jan 2007, low but watched for inflection
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the mean return conditional on the trigger is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and elevated federal funds rates constitute a stagflationary supply-demand imbalance that compresses profit margins and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening with slowing industrial activity
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and FEDFUNDS does not significantly predict 60-day S&P 500 returns (t-stat magnitude < 2.0) in a multivariate regression over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, an acceleration in consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain or increase the federal funds rate, raising real discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds held at 5.25% since June 2006 pause
failure_condition: In regimes where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, S&P 500 returns following CPIAUCSL accelerations are not significantly negative (mean return >= 0 or t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained declines in retail and food services sales signal weakening household consumption expenditure, which accounts for the majority of US output and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 3-month RSAFS growth does not coincide with below-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered instances during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Changes in the federal funds rate directly alter the risk-free rate component of equity discount models; an unexpected increase in the target rate raises short-term funding costs and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: A 25 basis point increase in FEDFUNDS is not followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of instances within a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts are a leading indicator of residential investment and construction employment; a monthly decline in HOUST signals deteriorating cyclical demand that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1642.0 in Dec 2006, down from 2005 peaks
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in HOUST are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth captures the physical volume of goods produced in the economy; accelerating output indicates positive supply-side momentum that supports earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO do not coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of months during walk-forward evaluation.
